items.title: 5 Video Games Like Phasmophobia
items.summary: "Phasmophobia" has you and your friends locked in terror until all the horrors are conquered. Need more like it? These five games creep close to the goal.
items.content:
Kinetic Games Among the scariest games released within the past several years is "Phasmophobia," developed by the British studio Kinetic Games. The game is available to play solo or online cooperatively, with players controlling a team of ghost hunters. Each level has players investigate a haunted site to determine what kind of spirit is prowling the area. The game ended up getting fixed because it had too many scares as a testament to its overall fear factor.

The surprise success of "Phasmophobia" led to many team-based ghost-hunting games filling the market, several of which are quite good. These games each feature their own memorably haunted sites and unique ghosts to keep players on their toes. With that particular niche in mind, we're listing games that place players in the midst of the haunting as they try to determine what spectral activity they've found themselves up against. Here are five video games like "Phasmophobia," all picked to help keep the paranormal frights coming after you in the dark.

Kyle's Garage Launched in early access in 2021, the indie game "Obsideo" offers its own twist on team-based ghostly investigations. With a squad of anywhere from one to four players working together, the game has priests visit various haunted sites. Using their own tools and environmental clues to determine what kind of malevolent ghost is active in a given assignment. From there, players must properly carry out the appropriate exorcism ritual to purge the malicious spirit for good.

To be clear, "Obsideo" is still in its early access phase and has also gone without updates for long enough to be flagged as possibly abandoned. That makes it a cautious buy (and a sometimes temperamental one), with frustrated players rating it as "mixed" at present. But if you push through, "Phasmophobia" fans will recognize a similar setup to the game, with enough distinct features to keep the experience fresh. The inclusion of the exorcism mechanic is a welcome addition, not only distinguishing it from "Phasmophobia" but giving players the opportunity to fight back. Hopefully, "Obsideo" will get a final official release, but the early access shows a lot of promise and hews closely to the Kinetic Games title.

LostOneTeam Another game technically still within its early access phase is the 2022 title "Ghost Exile" by LostOneTeam. The game also has teams of up to four players working together to examine sites with suspected hauntings in desperate need of an exorcism. Like "Phasmophobia" and many other games on this list, players use environmental clues to determine what kind of phantasm they're facing. Using their equipment, the players then perform an exorcism, though there are lethally dire consequences if they misidentify the apparitions.

"Ghost Exile" brings the spectral terror and crime scene investigation elements to the forefront of its gameplay, but with its own twists. The game offers more customization options for players to make the experience feel truly their own, right down to character design. Like "Phasmophobia," the game also possesses a voice recognition mechanic, allowing players to communicate with ghosts during their examination. With ghosts that can level up and an unlockable skill tree, "Ghost Exile" brings a surprising amount of depth to its familiar premise.

Clock Wizard Games The 2023 game "Demonologist" dials up the sheer terror and supernatural menace compared to many of the other titles on this list. The game again has players either work alone or with teams of up to four to investigate paranormal locations. After identifying the types of apparitions that present around a given site, players can then work together to exorcise the lethal entities; familiar, but it works. The ghosts feel much more dangerous than they do in "Phasmophobia" as they will lash out and stalk players investigating them.

"Demonologist" is definitely one of those horror games that you should play during the spooky season . This is a game that heavily evokes a pervasively unsettling atmosphere and ramps up the suspense as the various spirits go on the offensive. To elevate the scares, the game features incredibly detailed environments and an immersive sound design, really putting players on edge. An even more intense take on a familiar premise, "Demonologist" is best played with friends, rather than taking on these hellish frights alone.

Dreambyte Games The 2024 game "Forewarned" has more of a grave robbing premise rather than one revolving around paranormal investigations. The game has teams of up to four players raid ancient Egyptian tombs for treasure, only to incur supernatural wrath at each burial site. The investigative elements that are present in the gameplay are used to identify the types of undead monsters prowling the sites. With this knowledge, players can exploit the mummies' strengths and weaknesses as they recover holy relics and escape in one piece.

With a cultural focus on Egyptian legends and some slightly silly stereotypes, "Forewarned" certainly stands out from other supernatural investigation games out on the market. The game's tomb raiding premise also helps differentiate it, while providing players with an alternative objective to other games on this list. Like "Phasmophobia," the game also enjoys virtual reality headset functionality, making it a particularly terrifying game to play in VR . A team-based game that does something much more distinct with the usual premise, "Forewarned" is a lot of fun when coordinating with friends over the immersive device.

Renderise After initially launching in an early access phase on Steam in 2022, "Ghost Watchers" got its official release in 2025. Like every other game on this list, the title allows up to four players working together to battle ghosts. Players explore haunted locations like abandoned houses, police stations, and ghost towns to determine what kind of paranormal activity is afoot. From there, gamers have a whole arsenal of tools to help them fight back against the deadly enemies and end their threat for good.

With dozens of unique enemy types and an immersive variety of environments to explore and purge of phantasms, "Ghost Watchers" reflects its years of hard work from the developers. Renderise has crafted a genuinely spooky experience that, like all the other games on this list, is best experienced in the company of friends. Because many of the enemies in this game are truly terrifying, purging them is all the more satisfying. A moody little ghost hunt, "Ghost Watchers" is the most recent title on this list and one well worth checking out.

items.title: Phasmophobia - Hotfix v0.17.1.5
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Today we have released a hotfix for Phasmophobia, addressing two issues raised by the community this week. The update is now available across all platforms. Patch notes: Fixed an issue that caused players to bec
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters.

Today we have released a hotfix for Phasmophobia, addressing two issues raised by the community this week. The update is now available across all platforms.

Patch notes:

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to become softlocked when using the Spirit Box while using a controller.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue for Steam players that caused Voice Recognition to not work.

    [/*]

  • If you are encountering issues with Voice Recognition on the "Voice" setting on PC, please ensure that the default microphone assigned on your PC is the microphone specified in your windows settings.

    [/*]

items.title: Phasmophobia v0.17.1.4 - Patch Notes
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Today we have released our latest update for Phasmophobia, addressing several known issues in the game. We are aware of the current ongoing issues in-game and are currently implementing fixes and improvements in
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters.

Today we have released our latest update for Phasmophobia, addressing several known issues in the game.

We are aware of the current ongoing issues in-game and are currently implementing fixes and improvements in future updates for Phasmophobia. We’ll be sharing our plans for the rest of 2026 later this month with you all, including a timeline for fixes and updates related to both the Player Character Update and game in general. In the meantime, please continue to provide feedback and bug reports so that our team can investigate as much as possible. You can submit any and all issues via our Support Centre.

New

  • The VR belt has been reimplemented following player feedback. Players will now also be able to switch between having their Journal and Walkie Talkie on the belt or their shoulder. 

    • The VR belt will only be visible locally, players who are looking at a VR player will see the equipment on their body instead.

      [/*]
    [/*]

Changes

  • The line of sight ghost speed accelerations implemented in the Player Character Update have been reverted to their original state.

    [/*]

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to become softlocked when using the Spirit Box before interacting with the Truck’s CCTV.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed the Kormos to kill players through floors.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that allowed the Kormos to kill players outside.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Tier 3 Firelight to not work correctly if placed while lit.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where player jackets would appear incorrectly when loading into a Multiplayer lobby.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where pressing ‘Join’ in Multiplayer without having a code entered would cause issues creating lobbies, and remove audio from the game.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where VR players were unable to use Text Input for the Spirit Box, Monkey Paw and Ouija Board.

    [/*]

Known Issues

  • Players across all platforms are experiencing issues with voice recognition/voice chat. A fix is being investigated.

    [/*]

Please note: Following this update, players on Windows 7/8 or other out-of-date Windows installs may need to run Windows Update (and restart) for voice chat to connect. This is due to a recent security certificate update on our voice servers. More info can be found here.

As you continue your investigations in Phasmophobia, if you encounter bugs, issues or simply have feedback for our team, please use our Support Centre to submit your reports.

items.title: Phasmophobia's Update 1.000.040 Released on June 2
items.summary: Phasmophobia update 1.000.040 is out on June 2, as Kinetic Games has promised future plans for 2026 will be revealed this month.
items.content:
Kinetic Games has released a new Phasmophobia update on June 2 for consoles and PC, and this addresses a few known issues, as well as apply changes based on player feedback.

While today’s patch, which console gamers will see as Phasmophobia update 1.000.040 , doesn’t address every issue, the developers are aware of ongoing player concerns and are working on it.

For the patch notes, as well as more info on the game’s future, read on.

There’s also a known issue regarding voice recognition/voice chat, and a fix is being investigated. Moreover, Kinetic has said they are going to reveal what their plans are for the rest of 2026 later this month, and this includes a timeline for fixes and updates related to both the Player Character Update and the game in general.

Once we know more about that, we’ll let our readers know.

RELATED Valve Veteran Worried About Bungie Because Sony No Longer “Cares” About the Studio After Destiny 2’s Final Update Bungie’s plans to release two Destiny 2 expansions every year came to an end when the studio announced that it was cancelling all future expansions and that the game’s final update would arrive in June.

Stay connected to MP1st and the latest news by following us on Bluesky , X , Facebook , TikTok , YouTube , and Google News .

Father, gamer, games media vet, writer of words, killer of noobs.

items.title: Phasmophobia by Alan Wake - v0.17.1.0
items.summary: Welcome bac K Ghost Hunters, a new story has been written. Today we are excited t O release Phasmophobia by Alan Wake , our fi R st in-ga M e collaborati O n event, created in partner S hip with Remedy Games. With the update now rolling out
items.content:
Welcome bacK Ghost Hunters, a new story has been written.

Today we are excited tO release Phasmophobia by Alan Wake, our fiRst in-gaMe collaboratiOn event, created in partnerShip with Remedy Games.

With the update now rolling out Across all platformS, we're excited for you to experience What's in store.

Below, you'll find our lAuNch trailer alonG with the patch notes.

New

  • Phasmophobia by Alan Wake: A writer, a story, a mystery. Uncover the secrets of the Dark Place in our one-time in-game event that brings the world of Alan Wake 2 to Phasmophobia. Unlock player cosmetics, solve the riddles of the Dark Place and much more.

    [/*]

  • Added a 5 second delay after you die to allow you to select a ghost in the Journal. (Singleplayer only)

    [/*]

Changes

  • Salt will now get instantly placed at the start of the animation instead of the end.

    [/*]

  • Most equipment hold poses have been changed to fix several visual issues including wrists, elbows, arms and the equipment's position on the screen.

    [/*]

  • The T3 Sanity Medication animation will no longer play in Multiplayer.

    [/*]

  • The Tier 1 Igniter is now open so you can see how many matches are left.

    [/*]

  • Removed the inventory swapping animation to allow for instant swapping of held equipment.

    [/*]

  • Prestige cosmetics are now free after they are unlocked based on your Prestige level.

    [/*]

Fixes

Character Model Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused the heads of character models to look incorrect when the player looks up and down.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused problems with the character rig upon being revived while crouched.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused character models to fall down incorrectly upon death.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Non-VR player character’s feet to clip below the ground when standing still.

    [/*]

  • The player characters feet will no longer float in some areas.

    [/*]

  • Fixed the characters walking animation when below 50% player speed.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused character model mouths to be open when the player moves to the right.

    [/*]

Ghost Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that prevented the Yurei from fully closing doors.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Raiju to flicker lights outside of Hunts.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on 42 Edgefield Road that caused the Ghost to get stuck in multiple rooms and not be able to reach players in the garage lockers.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where Ghosts in the Grafton Farmhouse Attic would wander too often outside their room.

    [/*]

Environment Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused doors to move incorrectly when holding the Tarot Cards.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused doors to be blocked by other players.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented some doors on Bleasdale Farmhouse from opening correctly.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused doors to become detached from their hinges.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Sunny Meadows Restricted to load into the full Sunny Meadows location.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused two boomboxes to be displayed in the lobby.

    [/*]

  • Fixed several issues that caused inconsistencies with the animations when grabbing objects.

    [/*]

Equipment Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused held equipment to stutter when turning.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Head Gear to be misaligned while crouching.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented players from being seen on the CCTV through Tier 1 Head Gear.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Tier 1 Firelight being blown out to be heard throughout the map.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Tier 2 Photo Camera audio to play when using other equipment.

    [/*]

  • Fixed several issues that prevented the Tier 2 and 3 Firelight from working correctly.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the CCTV screen to be displayed on a Video Camera when first grabbed.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused usability issues with the Head Gear after toggling night vision on/off on a Video Camera.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue related to the above that caused the Tripod to become invisible.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Ouija Board planchette to float.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the watch’s timer to desync upon death and revival.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Ouija Board and Haunted Mirror to drop on their sides.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the wrong Tarot Card to be seen by other players when drawn by the player using them.

    [/*]

  • Fixed several visual issues when grabbing an Igniter while holding a Flashlight.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Flashlights to become invisible when being dropped to pick up another item.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Flashlight to appear when holding the Journal and pressing using the Flashlight.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Spirit Box responses to be heard incorrectly

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused a still image to be displayed on the Tier 3 Video Camera when leaving the Van.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the wrong exterior doors to lock on Bleasdale Farmhouse.

    [/*]

VR Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that prevented VR players from opening the upstairs hallway left side closet door.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where VR Players had to grab objects twice to pick them up.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Head Gear to disappear from the player’s hands when grabbed.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused a VR player’s screen to spin when dying or reviving if immersive animations are enabled.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the audio of a VR player dying could be heard across the map.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused objects to appear incorrect in a player’s hand.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused items dropped by a VR player after they die to not appear for other players.

    [/*]

Other Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that prevented Voice Chat from working when the player was Dead.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that would cause the Journal to remain in the player’s hand while Dead, if it was opened while dying.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused misaligned text with photo names in the Journal.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the light from a Dead player’s Journal from being visible to players that are alive.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue with the Journal that made it difficult to navigate between pages and tabs.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented the “Welcome Back” audio from playing.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented the ‘’Doors Starting Open’’ custom difficulty setting from working correctly.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to hear Dead players opening and closing their Journals.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused a soft lock if Alt + Tab was used during the death animation.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented the Legacy Badge menu options from being selected.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the journal objectives tick colour to be incorrect.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused door sounds to play when not moving and be heard across the map.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the ID Card foil animation to not render correctly.

    [/*]

  • Various other fixes and improvements.

    [/*]

Known Issues

  • Visual issues may be experienced using the Angel Lamp while holding an EMF Reader.

    [/*]

  • Page dropping audio may be heard while solving Nursery Rhyme riddles in Multiplayer.

    [/*]

  • Photos taken with the Tier 1 Photo Camera by a player may appear incorrect in another player's Journal.

    [/*]

  • A slight delay in the EMF Reader animation, but no effect on gameplay (console only).

    [/*]

  • Slight visual issues with various equipment animations.

    [/*]

  • Slight visual issues with host's ID Card if another player leaves a lobby.

    [/*]

  • Tier 1 Spirit Box may become invisible on Oh Deer Diner if held while interacting with the Truck's CCTV.

    [/*]

items.title: Alan Wake Comes To Horror Game Phasmophobia
items.summary: This is a free update on all platforms.
items.content:
Kinetic It's a meeting of the terrifying minds. The horror survival game Phasmophobia just launched a three-week crossover event with the Alan Wake franchise. It's appropriately called Phasmophobia by Alan Wake and features reimagined maps and character skins for both Wake and co-protagonist Saga Anderson.

The game will now task players with retrieving pages of a book written by fictional novelist Alan Wake. These pages were actually written by Sam Lake, the creative director of Remedy and the lead story architect for the franchise. The updated maps let players explore locations inspired by the fantastic Alan Wake 2 .

The event is live until June 2. This is a free update that's available for all versions of Phasmophobia , including Steam, Xbox Series X/S, PS5 and PS VR2. While access to the new maps and story pages end at that date, the character skins last forever.

For the uninitiated, Phasmophobia is a co-op horror title in which players hunt ghosts in a variety of haunted locations. It's pretty fun and has become a fairly big hit . It's actually getting turned into a movie by Blumhouse.

The Alan Wake games are surreal single-player adventures set in a spooky version of the Pacific Northwest. This franchise is also getting the cinematic treatment in the near future.

items.title: Phasmophobia v0.17.1.0 - Patch Notes
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. We want to start today by apologising for our Player Character Update that launched last week. It’s clear we missed the mark on this, and didn’t deliver on our promises to you. Your feedback and your reviews wer
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters.

We want to start today by apologising for our Player Character Update that launched last week. It’s clear we missed the mark on this, and didn’t deliver on our promises to you. Your feedback and your reviews were justified. Our team has grown significantly over the last year, and with that comes a higher standard that, in this case, we failed to meet.

Tomorrow, May 12th, our next update for Phasmophobia will bring with it many fixes, iterations and changes to the game that bring things in-line with our vision for this update. While we know improvements can still be made, we believe this will provide a much better experience for everyone playing Phasmophobia. 

Over the past week, we’ve read feedback from across the entire community. The frustration and disappointment has been clear. Below is a look at the patch notes, with changes and fixes built on that feedback, that will go live with tomorrow’s update.

New

  • Added a 5 second delay after you die to allow you to select a ghost in the journal

    [/*]

Changes

  • Salt will now get instantly placed at the start of the animation instead of the end.

    [/*]

Most equipment hold poses have been changed to fix several visual issues including wrists, elbows, arms and the equipment's position on the screen.

  • The T3 Sanity Medication animation will no longer play in Multiplayer

    [/*]

  • The Tier 1 Igniter is now open so you can see how many matches are left

    [/*]

  • Removed the inventory swapping animation to allow for instant swapping of held equipment

    [/*]

  • Prestige cosmetics are now free after they are unlocked based on your prestige level

    [/*]

Fixes

Character Model Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused the heads of character models to look incorrect when the player looks up and down.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused problems with the character rig upon being revived while crouched.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused character models to fall down incorrectly upon death.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Non-VR player character’s feet to clip below the ground when standing still.

    [/*]

  • The player characters feet will no longer float in some areas

    [/*]

  • Fixed the characters walking animation when below 50% player speed

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused character model mouths to be open when the player moves to the right.

    [/*]

Ghost Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that prevented the Yurei from fully closing doors.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Raiju to flicker lights outside of Hunts.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue on 42 Edgefield Road that caused the Ghost to get stuck in multiple rooms and not be able to reach players in the garage lockers.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where Ghosts in the Grafton Farmhouse Attic would wander too often outside their room.

    [/*]

Environment Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused doors to move incorrectly when holding the Tarot Cards

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused doors to be blocked by other players.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented some doors on Bleasdale Farmhouse from opening correctly.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused doors to become detached from their hinges.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Sunny Meadows Restricted to load into the full Sunny Meadows location.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused two boomboxes to be displayed in the lobby.

    [/*]

  • Fixed several issues that caused inconsistencies with the animations when grabbing objects.

    [/*]

Equipment Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused held equipment to stutter when turning

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Head Gear to be misaligned while crouching.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented players from being seen on the CCTV through Tier 1 Head Gear.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Tier 1 Firelight being blown out to be heard throughout the map.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Tier 2 Photo Camera audio to play when using other equipment.

    [/*]

  • Fixed several issues that prevented the Tier 2 and 3 Firelight from working correctly.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the CCTV screen to be displayed on a Video Camera when first grabbed.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused usability issues with the Head Gear after toggling night vision on/off on a Video Camera.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue related to the above that caused the Tripod to become invisible.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Ouija Board planchette to float.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the watch’s timer to desync upon death and revival.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Ouija Board and Haunted Mirror to drop on their sides.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the wrong Tarot Card to be seen by other players when drawn by the player using them.

    [/*]

  • Fixed several visual issues when grabbing an Igniter while holding a Flashlight.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Flashlights to become invisible when being dropped to pick up another item.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the Flashlight to appear when holding the Journal and pressing using the Flashlight.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Spirit Box responses to be heard incorrectly

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused a still image to be displayed on the Tier 3 Video Camera when leaving the Van.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the wrong exterior doors to lock on Bleasdale Farmhouse.

    [/*]

VR Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that prevented VR players from opening the upstairs hallway left side closet door

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where VR Players had to grab objects twice to pick them up.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused Head Gear to disappear from the player’s hands when grabbed.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused a VR player’s screen to spin when dying or reviving if immersive animations are enabled

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the audio of a VR player dying could be heard across the map.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused objects to appear incorrect in a player’s hand.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused items dropped by a VR player after they die to not appear for other players.

    [/*]

Other Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that prevented Voice Chat from working when the player was Dead.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that would cause the Journal to remain in the player’s hand while Dead, if it was opened while dying.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused misaligned text with photo names in the Journal.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the light from a Dead player’s Journal from being visible to players that are alive.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue with the Journal that made it difficult to navigate between pages and tabs.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented the “Welcome Back” audio from playing.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented the ‘’Doors Starting Open’’ custom difficulty setting from working correctly.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused players to hear Dead players opening and closing their Journals.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused a soft lock if Alt + Tab was used during the death animation.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that prevented the Legacy Badge menu options from being selected.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the journal objectives tick colour to be incorrect

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused door sounds to play when not moving and be heard across the map

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue that caused the ID Card foil animation to not render correctly

    [/*]

While tomorrow’s update for Phasmophobia contains more than just these fixes and changes, it was important for us to share these with you to see what we’ve been working on while feedback has been coming in.

We’ve reviewed our roadmap and while we’ll provide an update on this later, we have made the decision to release a second Player Character Update with additional content and changes that will include:

  • Additional cosmetic options to allow you to further personalize your characters

    [/*]

  • Improved character animations, aimed at quality and speeding up the gameplay

    [/*]

  • Adding the legacy badge customisation to the shop customisation screen

    [/*]

  • Improving equipment usability and speed

    [/*]

  • Various quality of life improvements to the player character and overall gameplay

    [/*]

  • Many other new features, fixes and improvements

    [/*]

We know we now have a long journey ahead of us to earn back this community’s trust. We’ve begun to put plans in place to share more of our development processes with you on the way to 1.0, allowing you greater visibility into the work we’re doing and what you can expect from future updates for Phasmophobia. We ask that you stay tuned to our channels for more information on all of this in the future.

items.title: Phasmophobia - Player Character Update 0.17.0.0
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. Today we’ve released the Player Character Update for Phasmophobia. The update is out now on all platforms where Phasmophobia is available. Patch notes are available below. New Characters : 12 new, higher quality
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. 

Today we’ve released the Player Character Update for Phasmophobia. The update is out now on all platforms where Phasmophobia is available.

Patch notes are available below.

New

  • Characters: 12 new, higher quality character models (6 male, 6 female) with character bios, adjustable hair colour and more.

    [/*]

  • Cosmetics: A wide array of available cosmetic items to equip your chosen character with; coats, shirts and trousers, all with multiple colour options. Unique shirts for prestige and event specific achievements. Accessories such as piercings, hats and glasses are also available. Note: All cosmetics are either unlockable in-game or purchased with in-game currency.

    [/*]

  • Animations: Alongside the new character models, interactions with equipment, objects and the environment will now feature character animations. Upon dying, a new death animation will also be seen.

    [/*]

  • PC VR Players can enable/disable new first-person death animations with the ‘immersive animations’ toggle in the options menu, as seen below. If disabled, which is the default, the new death animation will be seen in third-person.

    [/*]

  • Player Watch: A watch has been added to the character models that can be used during contracts to monitor player sanity levels and keep track of time elapsed during the contract. To view the watch in full, players can hold the Use button when their left hand is empty or by opening their Journal. The watch will not display Sanity if the Sanity Monitor is disabled, nor will it count as a piece of electronic equipment for Ghost abilities.

    • For VR Players, the watch will always be visible and can be viewed by simply raising their left hand up.

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • Cursed Hollow Cosmetics: The Cursed Hollow cosmetic rewards are now available for all players. The Cursed Hollow Mask, T-Shirt and Necklace can be equipped from within the customisation Shop.

    [/*]

Changes

  • ID Cards: ID Cards and Badges are now equipped and swapped within the customisation Shop menu, rather than the main menu.

    [/*]

  • Death Room: The room a player will enter after being killed by the Ghost has been removed, with the player now seamlessly transitioning to the Dead state. 

    [/*]

  • Player’s Journal: The Journal will now appear in game when being used, rather than being a separate UI item.

    [/*]

  • The Prestige limit has been increased to 4000.

    •  Note: No additional unlocks are available after Prestige 20.

      [/*]
    [/*]

  • Physics have been added to all Doors, meaning they will now continue to move after the player lets go.

    [/*]

  • Flashlights will now be held in the player’s left hand, and can be dropped when the right hand is empty.

    [/*]

  • Equipment, such as the tripod, can now be placed while standing.

    [/*]

  • All items will now be placed on the character model, rather than invisible unless equipped.

    [/*]

  • Tier 2 Sound Recorders are now included in the Audio Only weekly challenge.

    [/*]

  • The lowest FOV option is now 70, rather than 60. The default setting remains at 70.

    [/*]

  • VR Specific Changes:

    • The inventory belt has been removed, with all items now placed on the character model.

      [/*]

    • The teleport movement circle will now render on top of other objects and will be invisible until the player presses the teleport button when at full stamina.

      [/*]
    [/*]

Fixes

  • Fixed an issue that caused Head Gear to not be visible on all FOV values.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in Prison where wishing for a trap with the Monkey’s Paw wouldn’t affect sliding doors.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Raiju’s hunt threshold would only increase if equipment was held.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Yokai could hear the Music Box from too far away.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue where the Jinn could drain sanity from a player in rooms above and below it.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR where a player’s Journal could be seen after that player had died.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR that caused player hands to shake.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR that caused difficulty picking up the Spirit Box from the van wall.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR that caused Salt to rotate with the player’s body, rather than hand.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR where players could interact with objects without teleport grab selected.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR where the player would be unable to scroll through the Journal in the lobby.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR where players would appear to slide down stairs when using teleport locomotion.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR where players could grab items through walls.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR that caused the Tier 3 Firelight to be held upside down.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR that caused the Tier 3 Firelight to float in mid-air for players in Multiplayer.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR that caused players to be able to teleport out of the Van at the end of the tutorial and skip areas.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR that made it difficult to interact with UI elements.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR that caused doors to push players aside.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR that caused the T1 Head Gear to fall off players when viewing the  CCTV.

    [/*]

  • Fixed an issue in VR that caused player’s models to become misaligned with their position.

    [/*]

  • Various other fixes and improvements.

    [/*]

The below are issues that we are aware of in-game and currently investigating a fix. In some cases, a fix is already planned for a future update. Please do submit any issues, outside of the below, you encounter to our team via our Support Centre as any and all information can help our team find a fix.

Known Issues

  • Players are unable to use the Journal while dying.

    [/*]

  • ‘Use’ animations may continue if the equipment is dropped mid-animation.

    [/*]

  • A drop in frame-rate occurs in the customisation menu.

    [/*]

  • Players will be invisible on CCTV through another player’s Tier 1 Head Gear.

    [/*]

  • Flashlights will remain in the player's hand when using a Tier 1 Photo Camera.

    [/*]

  • The Video Camera can be moved away from the player when switched out while in use.

    [/*]

  • The character model's eyes will be wide open after the player has died.

    [/*]

  • The custom difficulty setting “Doors starting open” will only work on Tanglewood.

    [/*]

  • Footstep audio will not correctly match the player's speed when sprinting or crouching.

    [/*]

  • Doors can be held shut by players holding items.

    [/*]

  • VR and Non-VR players can’t hear each others footsteps.

    [/*]

  • Various minor visual inconsistencies.

    [/*]

The following issues, as well as other known bugs, will be fixed with the launch of Phasmophobia's next update on May 12th, 2026: 

  • Items placed during a pickup animation will instead be placed at the previous placed position.

    [/*]

  • Pressing cancel on the hair customisation screen will apply the hair to the previously selected character.

    [/*]

  • Yurei’s door closing ability will not fully close the door.

    [/*]

  • Some ID Card badge icons become white if the player selects a badge that only has one unlock out of two.

    [/*]

  • Grabbing a Flashlight with a prop in-hand will cause the flashlight to be held at the player’s feet.

    [/*]

  • Meg Marr’s hair is bald when wearing a hat.

    [/*]

  • The Ouija Board and Haunted Mirror will drop sideways instead of flat on the ground.

    [/*]

  • Doors will not open correctly while holding the Tarot Cards.

    [/*]

  • If revived while crouching, the character rig will display incorrectly.

    [/*]

  • Head Gear will visually stutter when the player is crouched.

    [/*]

  • Equipment will visually stutter when the player is rotating.

    [/*]

  • Tier 3 Video Camera blocks too much of the screen on the right hand side.

    [/*]

  • Player model will ragdoll more than intended upon dying.

    [/*]

  • Hunted and Suburbanite descriptions have been corrected.

    [/*]

  • Animated ID Card & Badges will be displayed correctly.

    [/*]

  • VR Players feet will sink into the ground when crouched.

    [/*]

  • VR Players are unable to open the left hand closet door in the upstairs hallway of 10 Ridgeview Court.

    [/*]

We will also be making the following changes as part of the same update:

  • Removing the equipment switching animation to allow for instant switching.

    [/*]

  • Salt will be placed instantly instead of after the animation has finished.

    [/*]

  • Equipment will be moved closer to the screen for easier readability.

    [/*]

  • Player head rotation will now have a limit for looking up and down for Multiplayer, locally there is still no limit.

    [/*]

  • Various other visual and gameplay adjustments and improvements.

    [/*]

As you continue your investigations in Phasmophobia, if you encounter bugs, issues or simply have feedback for our team, please use our Support Centre to submit your reports.

items.title: Inside the Design of Phasmophobia’s Player Character Update
items.summary: Phasmophobia's Art Director goes into the thinking behind its extensive new player characters update, in anticipation of 1.0 later this year.
items.content:
This year, Phasmophobia is coming up to its sixth birthday in Early Access, and finally approaching its full 1.0 release. In that time, the game has grown and evolved in ways we couldn’t have imagined at the start. I joined early on in that journey, when things were still new and felt a little experimental. Since then, we’ve expanded and improved almost every part of the game, from new ghost types and equipment, to complete overhauls of existing maps. These improvements are all part of our final vision for the game when it releases into 1.0 this year, but some parts, such as the player character system, still remain largely unchanged.

We first started thinking about reworks, including the player character system, nearly five years ago with the intention of making the experience of playing Phasmophobia more realistic. Now, as we approach full launch, it felt like the right time to finally tackle it.

One of the biggest changes is also the simplest: you now have a body!

For the first time, players can now look down and see legs, move through environments in a way that feels more physical, and hold equipment properly. Equipment is now visible on your character when it’s not in your hands, so you can quickly see what your teammates are carrying without needing to ask. Also, the flashlights are no longer stuck in place as players have a left hand – which makes a bigger difference than you’d expect when moving through a dark place mid-investigation.

We wanted it to feel like you’re actually in the game rather than just floating through a space, as this will ultimately help players feel more immersed in the world.

We’ve also moved away from some of the ‘visual quirks’ of the old system. Loved by many, the ‘bendy backs’ and odd movements had their charm, but they could break the tension at the wrong moment. We of course want players laughing with each other, but not at what is happening on screen. The horror immersion works best when it is more realistic, and should make the scarier moments easier to digest without someone bending in half next to them.

Phasmophobia works best when you’re communicating, reacting, and figuring the investigation out together. When you can easily read what your teammates are doing and feel more present in the space, the experience clicks in a different way.

Customisation was another big focus. Before, you would often see multiple of the same character repeated in a lobby, and this system didn’t give much room for individual player identity. Players have loved expressing themselves with the existing ID cards backgrounds and badges feature, so with this rework, we wanted to open that up further, so investigations feel more personal.

There is now much more variety for customisable elements, which goes a long way in a co-op game. Hair colour and outfit options are just the start, and we’ll be expanding this over time with more choices.

Alongside this, animation has been a major area of improvement. We wanted movement to be more believable across the board whilst also giving certain moments more impact – especially death. The team have completely reworked the death animations: dying in a Phasmophobia game should feel like a whole moment in itself, and this is something we will continue to build on as we introduce new ghosts later on.

A lot of the work on this update has been behind the scenes, and it’s easily been one of the more complex tasks we’ve taken on. It’s been a big milestone for the team, and one we’ve been thinking about for years. Seeing it all come together has been incredibly rewarding, and it’s only the beginning of pushing the horror experience as we head towards 1.0 and beyond.

We’re really excited for players to jump in and experience these new moments, even if it means dying a little more and losing some of their hard-earned equipment along the way.

items.title: PHASMOPHOBIA’S PLAYER CHARACTER UPDATE LANDS IN-GAME
items.summary: Players can now customise their character for the first time, and experience all-new animations, including enhanced interactions for an even more thrilling...
items.content:
Press release — 5 May 2026 16:04 Share Share this story Copy link Link copied! Download

Players can now customise their character for the first time, and experience all-new animations, including enhanced interactions for an even more thrilling hunt!

SOUTHAMPTON, UK – May 5th, 2026 – Today, Kinetic Games is pleased to announce that the highly anticipated Player Character Update is now available in Phasmophobia , as a free update across all platforms. Ghost hunters will be able to experience more immersive hunts, with the update bringing a host of changes ahead of the game’s 1.0 launch, including completely re-imagined character models with new animations, customisation options and more.

One of the biggest changes the major overhaul brings is that players will now have a body. Teammates can now see what their fellow ghost hunters are holding without needing to ask, and players will be able to move through environments in ways that feel more physical.

New animations will also introduce more dynamic, realistic movements and remove quirks - like the bendy backs - for even scarier ghost hunts. Players can expect new death, revival and EMF animations, which were previously teased in Phasmophobia’s 2026 Roadmap.

Interactions have also been enhanced, with unique animations for equipment and objects, for an even more immersive experience. This includes the Journal, which will now feature in-game when used rather than as a separate UI item.

Alongside this, a new item, the player watch, can now also be accessed during investigations via the Journal or when the player’s left hand is empty. It will allow players to monitor sanity levels while also keeping track of time elapsed during a contract.

The update also expands personalisation, with 12 new character models to choose from, each customisable with clothing, hair colour, accessories and more.

All cosmetics can either be unlocked through gameplay challenges or purchased via the in-game store in the lobby, which will be entirely run on in-game currency, and will have new items added in future updates.

As part of the build up to its awaited 1.0 release, the update also introduces the addition of backstories for the player characters. Curious players will be able to discover a bit more about who the GHD company are, how they ended up working as ghost hunters, and why…

“Whilst the old character models have their own charm, the Player Character Update will make ghost hunts even more immersive,” said Corey Dixon Art Director at Kinetic Games “ Movements feel more realistic with new animations, and even though we’re saying goodbye to the bendy backs, we hope the extra scares make up for it!”

Phasmophobia’s Player Character Update is now available for free on all platforms. Phasmophobia is available in Early Access on PC via Steam , PS5, PS VR2 , and Xbox Series X|S , priced at $19.99/£16.75/€19.50.

Find out more about the Player Character Update on the blog post here , and find assets for this announcement here .

Founded in 2020, Kinetic Games is an independent development studio based in the UK. This small but mighty team’s goal is to create genre-defining games and build engaged communities around them. Kinetic Games’ first title, Phasmophobia, has sold over 28 million copies so far in Early Access thanks to an agile development team and a strong focus on player feedback.

items.title: ‘Phasmophobia’ “Player Character Update” Arrives Early May
items.summary: This week, developer Kinetic Games announced that their much anticipated "Player Character Update" to their long-running cooperative horror title
items.content:
News PlayStation Virtual Reality Xbox Genghis Husameddin

This week, developer Kinetic Games announced that their much anticipated “Player Character Update” to their long-running cooperative horror title ‘ Phasmophobia ‘ will launch on 5 May across all platforms. The major overhaul will see each ghost hunter reimagined, adding new animations, movements, and in-depth character customisation. Players can customise the new and improved character models with hairstyles, innerwear, outerwear, accessories, and more. All cosmetics can be unlocked through gameplay challenges or purchased via the new in-game store – entirely run on in-game currency, with no microtransactions.

Players had the chance to unlock new character customisation options during the 2026 Cursed Hollow event . Those who completed its limited-time missions will receive these exclusive items at launch, including a necklace, t-shirt, and Forest Spirit Mask.

Additional updates, such as the new death, revival, and EMF reader animations, were previously teased in Phasmophobia’s 2026 Roadmap, showcasing more dynamic, realistic movements as the game makes its way towards its 1.0 release. These are just some of the improvements arriving alongside further bug fixes on 5 May.

“Since Phasmophobia launched in 2020, the team has put in so much effort to make the game more immersive and replayable, and giving players the freedom to personalise their ghost hunters feels like the natural next step,” said Corey Dixon , Art Director at Kinetic Games. “Player customisation unlocks so many possibilities for us in the future, and paired with the updated models and animations we’re also starting to up the scare factor even more – which is just a small part of the bigger journey to 1.0.”

Phasmophobia is available in Early Access across PC via Steam , PlayStation 5, PS VR2 , and Xbox Series consoles, priced at 19.99 USD/16.75 GBP/19.50 EURO.

Enjoying DayOne ? Do us a favour, and make us a ‘ Preferred Source on Google ‘ – that way, you’ll only be delivered more gaming coverage from our team.

How do you feel about Phasmophobia? Head over and let us know your thoughts in the DayOne Forums , the perfect place to chat about games if you’re a little more old-school and prefer things in a longform format. If you prefer the chaos of live and fast moving conversation, come and make some noise in the lively DayOne Community Discord Server instead. If you’re rocking a DayOne Membership , both places give special access for our supporters, and you’ll unlock a ton of exclusive content , just for you. If you’re looking to be a bigger part of the amazing DayOne community, then either choice is a good one!

items.title: Player Character Update, coming May 5th, 2026.
items.summary: Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. We have some much-anticipated news for you. Today we’re excited to announce that Phasmophobia’s Player Character Update will be launching across all platforms on May 5th, 2026. Stay tuned to our social channels
items.content:
Welcome back, Ghost Hunters. We have some much-anticipated news for you.

Today we’re excited to announce that Phasmophobia’s Player Character Update will be launching across all platforms on May 5th, 2026. Stay tuned to our social channels and official Discord server for exact release timings. 

While we won’t go into too many details before the release, we did want to lift the curtain ever so slightly on some of what you can expect to see in the update. A full rundown of changes, additions and more will be available when the update is released.

Of course, at the heart of this update is the ability to customise your player character in-game. After a long wait, the Customisation shop in the lobby is finally open for business. Here you’ll be able to choose from one of 12 characters and be able to change their clothing, hair colour and much more. While the shop is full of options at launch, you can look forward to more items being added in future updates and Twitch Drops - such as the below High Priestess T-shirt drop that'll be starting with the launch of the update.

This Twitch Drop will be available for all Phasmophobia creators to distribute to their communities from May 5th until May 11th.

To earn Twitch Drops, make sure you've connected your Twitch account to Phasmophobia.

Customisation plays a huge role in this update, but it’s not the only change. With the Player Character Update, we’ll be introducing new character animations as well as some changes to the overall gameplay experience such as the removal of the death room and the way you interact with both the environment and your chosen equipment during an investigation.

Check out a few screenshots below to get a taste of what’s to come on May 5th.

There is plenty to explore and experience once the update launches and we can’t wait to hear your thoughts on the changes to the game. Of course, this is a huge update to the game we’ve all known and loved for many years so we’ll be looking out for feedback after release so that we can continue to iterate and improve Phasmophobia as we head towards 1.0 later this year.
